title: Health Promotion and Disease Prevention

learning_objectives.text: Define and differentiate between primordial, primary, secondary, and tertiary prevention levels
Describe key health promotion principles including the Ottawa Charter and Health in All Policies approach
Explain major behavior change theories and their applications in health promotion
Compare prevention frameworks across different health conditions and populations
Summarize the social ecological model and its application to health promotion interventions
Interpret cost-effectiveness analyses of prevention programs
Apply behavior change theories to design culturally appropriate health promotion interventions
Use prevention frameworks to develop comprehensive intervention strategies for specific health problems
Implement community mobilization approaches for health promotion
Analyze the effectiveness of population-level prevention interventions across different settings
Examine factors influencing the success of community health promotion programs
Assess the role of social determinants in health promotion intervention outcomes
Integrate multiple behavior change theories to design comprehensive health promotion programs
Develop implementation strategies for scaling up evidence-based prevention interventions
Create evaluation frameworks for complex health promotion initiatives
Evaluate the cost-effectiveness and sustainability of different prevention approaches
Critique health promotion programs using appropriate theoretical and methodological frameworks
Judge the quality and applicability of prevention research across different contexts

case_studies.title: Thailand's Tobacco Control Success
case_studies.outcomes: Significant reduction in smoking prevalence from 32% to 19% among adults
key_elements: Multi-pronged policy approach including taxation, advertising bans, and health warnings
Strong political leadership and civil society engagement

case_studies.title: North Karelia Project: Community CVD Prevention
case_studies.outcomes: 85% reduction in CVD mortality over 35 years
key_elements: Multiple intervention strategies targeting diet, smoking, and hypertension
Collaboration with local organizations and health services

case_studies.title: Mexico's Sugar-Sweetened Beverage Tax
case_studies.focus: Fiscal policy for obesity and diabetes prevention
case_studies.outcomes: 12% reduction in SSB purchases, particularly among low-income households

case_studies.title: Ethiopia's Health Extension Program
case_studies.outcomes: Improved maternal and child health outcomes, increased health service utilization
key_elements: Training of community health extension workers
Focus on preventive care and health education

items.question: Which level of prevention focuses on preventing the development of risk factors in healthy populations?
items.explanation: Primordial prevention aims to prevent the development of risk factors before they occur

items.question: According to the Health Belief Model, which factors influence health behavior change?
options: All of the above factors combined
items.correct_answer: All of the above factors combined
items.explanation: The Health Belief Model includes perceived susceptibility, severity, benefits, barriers, cues to action, and self-efficacy

items: Describe the five action areas of the Ottawa Charter for Health Promotion and provide an example of each
Explain how the social ecological model can be applied to design a comprehensive smoking cessation program
Compare and contrast the effectiveness of individual-level versus population-level prevention approaches
Design a multi-level intervention to prevent type 2 diabetes in a low-income urban community
Develop an implementation plan for scaling up a successful community-based hypertension control program
Analyze the cost-effectiveness of different approaches to reducing road traffic injuries

research_emphasis: Systematic reviews of prevention intervention effectiveness
Implementation science studies on scaling up prevention programs
Cost-effectiveness analyses of health promotion interventions
Community engagement and participatory evaluation research
Behavior change theory testing and application studies

quality_metrics.global_representation: All WHO regions and income levels represented
quality_metrics.theoretical_grounding: Multiple behavior change and health promotion theories
